title: Hotel WiFi looks fast — it won't hold a video call
note: Most hotels score 20–50 Mbps on SpeedTest. That reads fine, but shared mesh networks collapse under load. Test at your room desk before booking a Zoom call, not the lobby.

title: The islands are a different budget entirely
note: Koh Samui, Phi Phi, and Koh Tao run 40–60% more expensive than the mainland. Budget bungalows have mostly disappeared. Factor this in before planning a slow-travel month in the south.

title: Rainy season means cancelled transfers, not just wet days
note: June to October in the south brings real disruptions: ferries get cancelled for days at a time and roads to some areas flood. Build buffer days on both sides of any island stay.

title: ATM fees stack — withdraw the maximum each time
note: Most Thai ATMs charge a flat 220–250 THB fee per withdrawal regardless of amount. Take out the daily maximum rather than making small withdrawals throughout the week.

title: Air-con is often priced separately in guesthouses
note: Budget listings frequently show the fan-room price. Air-con adds 100–200 THB per night — ask before you commit, especially if you're travelling in March–May in the north.
